title.en: Reading 802.1CF: Scope, Purpose, and the Acronym Toolkit (Clauses 1 & 4)
title.zh: 读懂 802.1CF：范围、目的与术语工具箱（第 1 章与第 4 章）

blocks.en: Every IEEE standard opens with a few short clauses that tell you what the document is for and how to read the rest of it. IEEE Std 802.1CF-2019 is a Recommended Practice (a guideline, not a mandatory standard) for the Network Reference Model (NRM) of an IEEE 802 access network. Before you dive into the heavy architecture chapters, Clause 1 (Overview) answers two questions — what is in scope and why it exists — and Clause 4 (Acronyms, abbreviations, and conventions) hands you the vocabulary and notation you'll need on every later page. This lesson is a reference-grade walkthrough of those two clauses.
blocks.zh: 每一份 IEEE 标准开头都有几个简短的章节，告诉你这份文档是做什么的、以及该如何阅读后面的内容。IEEE Std 802.1CF-2019 是一份“推荐性规程（Recommended Practice，即指南而非强制标准）”，它定义了 IEEE 802 接入网的“网络参考模型（NRM, Network Reference Model）”。在你深入那些厚重的架构章节之前，第 1 章（Overview，概述）回答两个问题——什么在范围之内、为什么要有它——而第 4 章（缩略语、缩写与约定）则交给你后面每一页都会用到的词汇与记法。本课就是对这两章的参考级讲解。

blocks.en: Clause 1.1 (Scope) draws the boundary of the document. It states that 802.1CF specifies an access network — the part of the network that connects terminals (the end-user devices) to their access routers — using technologies from the IEEE 802 family (Ethernet, Wi-Fi, and so on). To do that it provides a Network Reference Model (NRM): a map of the entities (the boxes, like terminal, node of attachment, access router) and the reference points (the labeled interfaces between them), plus behavioral and functional descriptions of how those entities talk to each other. In short: Scope tells you the standard is about the link-layer 'on-ramp' between a device and the router that hands it off to the wider Internet.
blocks.zh: 第 1.1 章（Scope，范围）划定了文档的边界。它说明 802.1CF 规定的是一个“接入网（access network）”——也就是把“终端（terminal，即最终用户设备）”连接到其“接入路由器（access router）”的那一段网络——并使用 IEEE 802 家族的技术（以太网、Wi-Fi 等）。为此它提供了一个“网络参考模型（NRM）”：一张描绘各类“实体（entity，如终端、附着节点、接入路由器这些框）”和“参考点（reference point，框之间带标号的接口）”的地图，并配上这些实体之间如何通信的行为与功能描述。一句话：范围告诉你，这份标准讲的是设备与把它接入更广阔互联网的路由器之间、那段链路层的“上匝道”。

blocks.en: Two terms from Clause 3 (Definitions) make Scope click: a terminal is the device that terminates connections (your phone, laptop, IoT sensor); an access router is the router that terminates the MAC (medium access control) service from terminals and forwards their traffic by IP (Internet Protocol) address. The access network is everything in between.
blocks.zh: 第 3 章（Definitions，定义）里的两个术语能让“范围”一下子清晰：“终端（terminal）”是终结连接的设备（你的手机、笔记本、物联网传感器）；“接入路由器（access router）”是终结来自终端的 MAC（介质访问控制，medium access control）服务、并按 IP（互联网协议，Internet Protocol）地址转发其流量的路由器。而“接入网”就是夹在两者之间的全部部分。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 520 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><rect x="10" y="40" width="90" height="40" rx="4"/><text x="55" y="65" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Terminal</text><rect x="150" y="40" width="140" height="40" rx="4"/><text x="220" y="65" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Access Network</text><rect x="340" y="40" width="100" height="40" rx="4"/><text x="390" y="65" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Access Router</text><rect x="450" y="40" width="60" height="40" rx="4" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="480" y="58" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">Info</text><text x="480" y="72" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">Server</text><line x1="100" y1="60" x2="150" y2="60"/><line x1="290" y1="60" x2="340" y2="60"/><line x1="440" y1="60" x2="450" y2="60"/><text x="220" y="30" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Scope of 802.1CF (NRM)</text><line x1="10" y1="95" x2="440" y2="95" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/></svg>

caption.en: The 802.1CF Scope: the access network bridges terminals to access routers; the router then forwards by IP to information servers. The dashed span marks what the NRM covers.
caption.zh: 802.1CF 的范围：接入网把终端桥接到接入路由器；路由器再按 IP 把流量转发给信息服务器。虚线跨度标出 NRM 所覆盖的部分。

blocks.en: Clause 1.2 (Purpose) explains why the document exists. Modern networks are heterogeneous: a single user terminal may carry multiple network interfaces, support multiple access technologies, and hold multiple network subscriptions all at once (think of a phone juggling Wi-Fi, cellular fallback, and a private VPN). 802.1CF exists to support the design and deployment of such IEEE-802-based access networks, to guide developers who are extending existing standards to handle this heterogeneity, and to enable IEEE 802 technologies in brand-new deployments by clearly specifying their functions inside an access network. Where Scope draws the boundary, Purpose states the payoff: a common reference model so different link technologies can be described, controlled, and combined coherently.
blocks.zh: 第 1.2 章（Purpose，目的）说明文档为何存在。现代网络是“异构（heterogeneous）”的：一台用户终端可能同时拥有多个网络接口、支持多种接入技术、并持有多个网络订阅（想想一部手机同时玩转 Wi-Fi、蜂窝回退和一条私有 VPN）。802.1CF 的存在是为了：支撑这类基于 IEEE 802 的接入网的设计与部署；指导那些为应对异构性而扩展现有标准的开发者；并通过清晰规定 IEEE 802 技术在接入网中的功能，使其能用于全新的网络部署。如果说“范围”划定了边界，那么“目的”说明了收益：一套统一的参考模型，让不同的链路技术能够被一致地描述、控制和组合。

blocks.en: Clause 4 switches from 'why' to 'how to read.' Clause 4.1 (Acronyms and abbreviations) is the standard's master glossary — a flat list expanding every shorthand the rest of the document uses. You don't memorize it; you keep a finger in it. A handful are worth knowing up front because they name the model's core actors and services: NRM (network reference model), NA (node of attachment — a generic term for an access point, base station, or bridge where a terminal attaches), AN (access network), AR (access router), TE (terminal), SS (subscription service), and AAA (authentication, authorization, and accounting). Notice the pattern: many actors also get an -ID twin (AN-ID, AR-ID, NA-ID, TE-ID) — the identifier used to refer to that thing.
blocks.zh: 第 4 章从“为什么”切换到“怎么读”。第 4.1 章（Acronyms and abbreviations，缩略语与缩写）是这份标准的总词汇表——一张平铺的清单，展开文档其余部分所用的每一个简写。你不必背它，只需随时翻得到它。有几个值得先记住，因为它们命名了模型的核心角色与服务：NRM（网络参考模型）、NA（附着节点，node of attachment——对接入点、基站或终端所连桥接设备的统称）、AN（接入网）、AR（接入路由器）、TE（终端）、SS（订阅服务，subscription service）以及 AAA（认证、授权与计费，authentication, authorization, and accounting）。注意一个规律：许多角色还配有一个 -ID 的孪生项（AN-ID、AR-ID、NA-ID、TE-ID）——即用来指代该事物的标识符。

blocks.en: The acronym list quietly reveals 802.1CF's neighbors. You'll spot the SDN/NFV control vocabulary (SDN software-defined networking, NFV network function virtualization, NETCONF, SNMP), the 802.1 time-sensitive networking trio (TSN, plus CNC Centralized Network Configuration and CUC Centralized User Configuration), and the Wi-Fi identifiers (SSID, BSSID, HESSID). Seeing which acronyms appear tells you which worlds this access-network model is designed to bridge.
blocks.zh: 这张缩略语清单悄悄透露了 802.1CF 的“邻居”。你会看到 SDN/NFV 的控制词汇（SDN 软件定义网络、NFV 网络功能虚拟化、NETCONF、SNMP），看到 802.1 时间敏感网络三件套（TSN，外加 CNC 集中式网络配置、CUC 集中式用户配置），还有 Wi-Fi 标识符（SSID、BSSID、HESSID）。看清出现了哪些缩略语，就能知道这套接入网模型意在桥接哪些世界。

blocks.en: Clause 4.2 (Conventions) defines two pieces of notation you must read literally throughout the standard. Clause 4.2.1 (Occurrence of information elements) gives a cardinality shorthand — how many times an attribute may appear in a data structure. There are exactly four symbols: {0+} means zero or more instances are present, {0-1} means zero or one (i.e., optional, at most once), {1} means exactly one (mandatory, single), and {1+} means one or more (mandatory, repeatable). When a later clause writes an attribute with one of these braces, it is stating an iron rule about how many of that thing the information model allows.
blocks.zh: 第 4.2 章（Conventions，约定）定义了两套你在全书中必须照字面理解的记法。第 4.2.1 章（Occurrence of information elements，信息元素的出现次数）给出一种“基数（cardinality）”简写——某个属性在数据结构中可以出现多少次。一共只有四个符号：{0+} 表示出现零个或多个实例；{0-1} 表示零个或一个（即可选、至多一次）；{1} 表示恰好一个（必选、单个）；{1+} 表示一个或多个（必选、可重复）。当后面的章节给某个属性标注其中一个花括号时，它就是在声明信息模型对“这个东西能有几个”所定下的铁律。

blocks.en:
flowchart LR
  A["Attribute in model"] --> B{"How many?"}
  B -->|"{0+}"| C["zero or more"]
  B -->|"{0-1}"| D["optional, max 1"]
  B -->|"{1}"| E["exactly one"]
  B -->|"{1+}"| F["one or more"]
blocks.zh:
flowchart LR
  A["模型中的属性"] --> B{"出现几次?"}
  B -->|"{0+}"| C["零个或多个"]
  B -->|"{0-1}"| D["可选, 至多一个"]
  B -->|"{1}"| E["恰好一个"]
  B -->|"{1+}"| F["一个或多个"]

blocks.en: Clause 4.2.2 (Information model notation) sets the drawing language for the rest of the document: the information model is expressed in UML 2.x object model notation. UML (Unified Modeling Language) is the standard set of boxes-and-lines used to depict objects, their attributes, and the associations between them. 802.1CF uses only a subset of UML, and that subset is illustrated in Annex B for reference. Practically, this means when you reach the model diagrams later in the standard, you should read them as UML class/object diagrams — not as network topology pictures — and the occurrence braces from 4.2.1 are what decorate the associations on those diagrams.
blocks.zh: 第 4.2.2 章（Information model notation，信息模型记法）为全书其余部分设定了“绘图语言”：信息模型以 UML 2.x 对象模型记法表示。UML（统一建模语言，Unified Modeling Language）是一套标准的“框与线”，用来描绘对象、对象的属性以及对象之间的关联。802.1CF 只使用 UML 的一个子集，并在附录 B（Annex B）中给出该子集以供参考。这在实践中意味着：当你读到标准后面的模型图时，应把它们当作 UML 类图/对象图来看——而不是网络拓扑图——而 4.2.1 的出现次数花括号，正是装点这些图中各条关联线的标注。

summary.en: Coverage map — exact clause numbers taught here
summary.zh: 覆盖地图——本课所讲的确切章节编号

blocks.en: This lesson teaches all five units of the standard's opening matter, each on a page that was read directly: (1.1) Scope, p.17 — 802.1CF specifies an IEEE-802-based access network connecting terminals to access routers, delivered as a Network Reference Model (NRM) of entities and reference points with behavioral/functional descriptions. (1.2) Purpose, p.17 — supports design and deployment of heterogeneous IEEE 802 access networks (multiple interfaces, technologies, subscriptions in one terminal), guides standards extensions, and enables IEEE 802 tech in new deployments. (4.1) Acronyms and abbreviations, pp.20-21 — the master glossary expanding every shorthand (AAA, NA, AN, AR, TE, SS, NRM, SDN, NFV, TSN/CNC/CUC, SSID/BSSID, and the -ID identifier family). (4.2.1) Occurrence of information elements, p.21 — the four cardinality symbols {0+}, {0-1}, {1}, {1+}. (4.2.2) Information model notation, p.21 — the model uses a UML 2.x subset, illustrated in Annex B.
blocks.zh: 本课讲授标准开篇部分的全部五个单元，每一个都对应一页直接读过的原文：（1.1）范围，第 17 页——802.1CF 规定一个基于 IEEE 802、把终端连到接入路由器的接入网，以“网络参考模型（NRM）”形式给出实体与参考点及其行为/功能描述。（1.2）目的，第 17 页——支撑异构 IEEE 802 接入网（一台终端含多接口、多技术、多订阅）的设计与部署，指导标准扩展，并使 IEEE 802 技术可用于全新部署。（4.1）缩略语与缩写，第 20-21 页——展开每一个简写的总词汇表（AAA、NA、AN、AR、TE、SS、NRM、SDN、NFV、TSN/CNC/CUC、SSID/BSSID，以及 -ID 标识符家族）。（4.2.1）信息元素的出现次数，第 21 页——四个基数符号 {0+}、{0-1}、{1}、{1+}。（4.2.2）信息模型记法，第 21 页——模型采用 UML 2.x 的一个子集，见附录 B。

cites.note: Clause 4.1 acronym list; 4.2.1 occurrence notation {0+}/{0-1}/{1}/{1+}; 4.2.2 UML 2.x information model notation.
